Disclaimer: Call 911 in case of emergency; 988 for suicide prevention lifeline; This is my first-person account of my own journey; This is not advice. Full acceptable use policy.

Things That Worked

Strategies, activities, and approaches that helped or continue to help my sobriety.

← Back to Recovery Life Skills
This is my own personal list — not a recommendation or medical advice. What worked for me may not work for you. Every person in recovery is different. These are the specific things that have helped my sobriety over 9+ years.

Quick Strategies

Practical things I kept in mind day to day:

  1. Sugar
  2. Chocolate
  3. Coffee
  4. Keep some candy in pocket
  5. Avoid hand sanitizer
  6. Reach out to your sponsor every day
  7. Go to meetings every day
  8. Take a different path home
  9. Never go to your old bar
  10. Take an Uber if unable to drive
  11. Go to a meeting immediately when a burning desire hits
  12. Call sponsor immediately when a burning desire hits
  13. Get help ASAP for burning desires

Activities and Approaches

A broader set of activities I incorporated into my program:

  1. Attending AA Meetings
    • Discussion
    • Speaker
    • Big Book
    • Home Group
    • Service Work
    • Upon Awakening
    • Took notes and did research when I got home
  2. Audio Sobriety Recordings
    • Speaker Recordings
    • Podcasts
    • Sobriety Videos
  3. Therapy
    • Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT)
    • Talk Therapy
    • Group Therapy
  4. Bible Study
    • Podcasts
    • Videos
    • Reading
    • Audio Reading
  5. 12 Steps
    • Upon Awakening
    • Handing our flaws over to God
    • Traditional AA sponsorship
  6. Meditation
    • Solo Meditation
    • Solo Prayer
    • Group Meditation and Prayer
    • Devotion
  7. Church
    • Bible Studies
    • Christian Support Groups
    • Church Services
    • Church Service Projects
    • Volunteer Work
  8. Medical
    • Prescriptions
    • Psychiatrist
  9. Vitamins
    • B Complex
    • Electrolytes
    • A few other options
  10. Medications
    • Antabuse
    • A few newer options
  11. Community
    • Support Groups
    • Community Service Projects
    • Volunteer Work
  12. Professional Facilities
    • Detox Center
    • In-Patient Rehab Treatment
    • Out-Patient Rehab Treatment
    • Sober Living
    • Respite
  13. Recovery Groups
    • Celebrate Recovery
    • Alcoholics Anonymous
    • SMART Recovery
  14. Sponsorship and Peer Support
    • Continue to call and leave message or talk to sponsor every day
    • Started having sponsees calling every day and talk to sponsor when help is needed
    • Peer Support (See CPS-AD)
  15. Personal Health
    • Exercise
    • Healthy Food
    • Hydration
    • Consistent Sleep
  16. Alternatives
    • Sugar
    • Chocolate
    • Coffee
    • Keep some candy in pocket
  17. New Friends
    • Find in AA
    • Find in Support Groups
  18. New Hobby or Skill
    • Sugar
    • Chocolate
    • Coffee
    • Keep some candy in pocket
  19. Animals
    • Therapy Cats
    • Service Dogs

Situation-Specific Strategies

My list of 100+. Not advice.

  1. Ask AI
    • List of excuses
    • Chocolate
  2. Attending AA Meetings
    • Avoid hand sanitizer
    • Reach out to your sponsor every day
    • Go to meetings every day
  3. Do Not Go to Work Outings with Alcohol
    • Sugar
    • Chocolate
    • Coffee
    • Keep some candy in pocket
  4. Driving
    • Take a different path home
  5. Food
    • Never go to your old bar
  6. Burning Desire
    • Take an Uber if unable to drive
    • Go to a meeting immediately
    • Call sponsor immediately
    • Get help ASAP
